div.span:
Keep malicious GPO active
Rename to look legitimate

Create shadow admin account
via GPO on all machines

Disable logging via GPO
Prevent detection

Scheduled task persistence
Executes even if GPO removed

GPO changes replicated
to all DCs via SYSVOL

Wait for scheduled gpupdate
Default: Every 90 min + random 30 min

Run on target:
gpupdate /force
or wait for reboot

Trick admin into running
gpupdate on target machines

1. Edit GPO via GPMC or PowerShell
2. Add Immediate Scheduled Task
3. Set action: Add user to local admins
4. Wait for gpupdate or force it
5. Compromised machines grant admin access

1. Access SYSVOL share
2. Navigate to GPO folder:
\\\\domain\\SYSVOL\\domain\\Policies\\{GUID}
3. Modify/create startup script
4. Inject payload:
net user backdoor P@ss123 /add
net localgroup administrators backdoor /add
5. Wait for machine reboot or gpupdate

1. Edit GPO preferences
2. Add registry key modification
3. Disable security features:
Windows Defender, Firewall, UAC
4. Push to all machines in OU
5. Machines become vulnerable

1. Search SYSVOL for cpassword:
findstr /S /I cpassword \\\\domain\\sysvol\\*.xml
2. Extract GPP password (Groups.xml)
3. Decrypt with known AES key
4. Password often for local admin
5. Use across multiple machines

Escalate privileges or
find different attack path

Admin access to all machines
in affected OU

Remove GPO after execution
Restore original settings

Keep GPO for persistent access
Monitor for admin detection

DETECTION INDICATORS:
Event 5136: GPO modification - Event 4662: GPO object access
SYSVOL file changes - Unusual .pol or .xml edits
gpupdate from unusual accounts - Off-hours GPO changes
New scheduled tasks appearing domain-wide
